en.extract:
The dusky pigeon (Patagioenas goodsoni) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is found in Colombia and Ecuador.


Taxonomy and systematics
The dusky pigeon is most closely related to the ruddy pigeon (P. subvinacea), short-billed pigeon (P. nigrirostris), and plumbeous pigeon (P. plumbia), and they have sometimes been placed in subgenus Oenoenas. The dusky pigeon is monotypic.


Description
The dusky pigeon is about 24 cm (9.4 in) long. Its head, throat, and breast are gray or purplish gray. Its nape is gray glossed with purple. Its shoulders, back, and rump are olive brown or dark purplish brown. The wings and tail are bronzy gray, sometimes glossed with purple. Its eye can be any of several shades of red.


Distribution and habitat
The dusky pigeon is found in the Chocó Biogeographic Region from Colombia near the Panamanian border south to the Río Palenque in northwestern Ecuador. It inhabits rainforest, mostly in lowlands but as high as 1,500 m (4,900 ft) in elevation.


Behavior


Feeding
No information on the dusky pigeon's foraging behavior or diet has been published.


Breeding
Specimens of dusky pigeon in breeding condition were taken between January and May and also in August, but no further information about the species' breeding phenology has been published.


Vocalization

The dusky pigeon's song is "a repeated phrase of three rather high-pitched coos whoah..pup..pup. Its call is "a purring, drawn out rrrrow.


Status
The IUCN has assessed the dusky pigeon as being of Least Concern. Though it has a restricted range, it is common locally within it. However, its life history is almost unknown, and "deforestation may prove to be a serious threat."


References

The '''dusky pigeon''' ('''''Patagioenas goodsoni''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is found in [[Colombia]] and [[Ecuador]].<ref name=IOC11.2>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/ |title=IOC World Bird List (v 11.2) |last1=Gill |first1= F. |last2=Donsker|first2=D.|last3=Rasmussen |first3=P. |date=July 2021 |access-date=July 14, 2021 }}</ref>

==Taxonomy and systematics==

The dusky pigeon is most closely related to the [[ruddy pigeon]] (''P. subvinacea''), [[short-billed pigeon]] (''P. nigrirostris''), and [[plumbeous pigeon]] (''P. plumbia''), and they have sometimes been placed in subgenus ''Oenoenas''.<ref name=SACClist>Remsen, J. V., Jr., J. I. Areta, E. Bonaccorso, S. Claramunt, A. Jaramillo, D. F. Lane, J. F. Pacheco, M. B. Robbins, F. G. Stiles, and K. J. Zimmer. Version 24 August 2021. A classification of the bird species of South America. American Ornithological Society. https://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCBaseline.htm retrieved August 24, 2021</ref> The dusky pigeon is [[monotypic]].<ref name=IOC11.2/>

==Description==

The dusky pigeon is about {{convert|24|cm|in|abbr=on}} long. Its head, throat, and breast are gray or purplish gray. Its nape is gray glossed with purple. Its shoulders, back, and rump are olive brown or dark purplish brown. The wings and tail are bronzy gray, sometimes glossed with purple. Its eye can be any of several shades of red.<ref name=DUPI-BOW>Baptista, L. F., P. W. Trail, H. M. Horblit, and P. F. D. Boesman (2020). Dusky Pigeon (''Patagioenas goodsoni''), version 1.0. In Birds of the World (J. del Hoyo, A. Elliott, J. Sargatal, D. A. Christie, and E. de Juana, Editors). Cornell Lab of Ornithology, Ithaca, NY, USA. https://doi.org/10.2173/bow.duspig2.01 retrieved September 14, 2021</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==

The dusky pigeon is found in the [[Tumbes–Chocó–Magdalena|Chocó Biogeographic Region]] from Colombia near the Panamanian border south to the [[Palenque River|Río Palenque]] in northwestern Ecuador. It inhabits [[rainforest]], mostly in lowlands but as high as {{convert|1500|m|ft|abbr=on}} in elevation.<ref name=DUPI-BOW/>

==Behavior==
===Feeding===

No information on the dusky pigeon's foraging behavior or diet has been published.<ref name=DUPI-BOW/>

===Breeding===

Specimens of dusky pigeon in breeding condition were taken between January and May and also in August, but no further information about the species' breeding [[phenology]] has been published.<ref name=DUPI-BOW/>

===Vocalization===

{{birdsong|url=https://www.xeno-canto.org/species/Patagioenas-plumbea |species=plumbeous pigeon}}
The dusky pigeon's song is "a repeated phrase of three rather high-pitched coos ''whoah..pup..pup''. Its call is "a purring, drawn out ''rrrrow''.<ref name=DUPI-BOW/>

==Status==

The [[IUCN]] has assessed the dusky pigeon as being of Least Concern.<ref name=IUCN/> Though it has a restricted range, it is common locally within it. However, its life history is almost unknown, and "deforestation may prove to be a serious threat."<ref name=DUPI-BOW/>
